programs.name: Elderly and Persons with Physical Disabilities (EPD) Waiver Program
programs.what_it_does: The EPD Waiver allows DC residents who would otherwise require nursing home care to receive services and supports while living in their home or assisted living communities. It provides home and community-based services as an alternative to institutional care.[5]
programs.who_its_for: DC residents aged 60+ or adults with physical disabilities who meet nursing facility level of care criteria and have income/asset limits.
programs.whats_unique: DC-funded waiver program expanding Medicaid to provide long-term home care services not universally available through federal Medicaid in every state.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by assessed needs; covers personal care, homemaker services, adult day care, and more up to nursing home equivalent costs.

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiaries (QMB) Program
programs.what_it_does: The QMB program provides secondary insurance coverage to Medicare beneficiaries, paying Medicare premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance. It acts as a wraparound to Medicare for low-income seniors.[5]
programs.who_its_for: DC Medicare beneficiaries with income below $3,418/month (single) or $4,598/month (couple) and limited assets.
programs.whats_unique: DC Medicaid-administered program with specific income thresholds higher than some states, fully state-funded for eligible non-Medicaid seniors.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of Medicare Part A/B premiums (up to $174.70/month Part B in 2024), deductibles ($240 Part B), and 100% coinsurance.

programs.name: DC State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free health insurance information, counseling, education, and assistance to Medicare beneficiaries, families, and caregivers. It helps navigate Medicare options, appeals, and supplemental coverage.[5]
programs.who_its_for: All DC Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+, their families, and caregivers regardless of income.
programs.whats_unique: DC-specific federally supported but locally administered counseling service tailored to DC's senior population and programs.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; potential savings of thousands annually through proper Medicare plan selection.

programs.name: DACL Senior Wellness Centers
programs.what_it_does: The centers promote health and wellness through programs, activities, and services for seniors. They had average enrollment of 197 to 2,400 individuals in FY 2018-2019.[3]
programs.who_its_for: DC seniors typically aged 60+ , with some programs means-tested.
programs.whats_unique: DC government-funded network of wellness centers focused on preventive health, unique in scope within the 90 DC senior programs.
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost access to health screenings, fitness, meals, and socialization; serves thousands annually.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides legal services, advice, and guidance for seniors facing issues like housing, benefits, or consumer problems. Offered through DACL to support vulnerable older adults.[5]
programs.who_its_for: DC seniors aged 60+ needing legal assistance, prioritized for low-income.
programs.whats_unique: DC-specific program delivering targeted legal aid to seniors, part of DACL's integrated aging services not replicated in all jurisdictions.
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations and representation; prevents evictions, secures benefits worth thousands.

programs.name: Community Transition Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides guidance, consultation, coordination for home and community-based services, screenings, assessments, and transitions from nursing homes/rehab in DC, MD, VA. Facilitates move to community living.[5]
programs.who_its_for: DC seniors and adults with disabilities transitioning from institutional care.
programs.whats_unique: DC-funded program coordinating regional transitions with unique tri-state focus for DC residents.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive support package; enables community placement saving ~$100K/year vs. nursing home costs.
